name: sage-solver-mcp
description: LLM-native optimization solver: LP, MIP, portfolio & scheduling via HiGHS/OR-Tools.
long_description:
SAGE gives Claude certified mathematical optimization capabilities using open-source solvers.

Solve linear programs (LP), mixed-integer programs (MIP), portfolio optimization, and workforce scheduling problems directly from conversation. SAGE returns optimal solutions with variable values, objective values, sensitivity analysis, and plain-English explanations.

When a problem is infeasible, SAGE computes the Irreducible Infeasible Subsystem (IIS) — the minimal conflicting constraint set — and ranks relaxation suggestions so you can fix the model quickly.

All computation runs locally. No data leaves your machine.

tools.name: solve_optimization
tools.description: Solve an LP, MIP, portfolio, or scheduling optimization problem. Returns optimal variable values, objective, solve time, and plain-English explanation. Returns IIS if infeasible.

tools.name: read_data_file
tools.description: Read an Excel (.xlsx) or CSV file and return a structured preview of its sheets, columns, and first 5 rows.

tools.name: solve_from_file
tools.description: Read data from an Excel/CSV file, solve the optimization model, and write results to a new Excel file. Returns solution summary and output path.

tools.name: explain_solution
tools.description: Get a natural-language explanation of the most recent solve result at brief, standard, or detailed depth.

tools.name: check_feasibility
tools.description: Check if an optimization problem has a feasible solution. If infeasible, returns the IIS and ranked relaxation suggestions.

tools.name: generate_template
tools.description: Generate a blank analyst-friendly Excel template for portfolio, scheduling, transport, or generic_lp problem types.

tools.name: suggest_relaxations
tools.description: For the most recent infeasible result, return ranked constraint relaxations (amount, percentage, trade-off explanation) that restore feasibility.
